grade_rationale: Clean architecture, well-scoped customization surface, exemplary validation script with tests. Zero broken items. The vague_terms merge-chain bypass and missing headless input contract are the two most actionable findings. Most opportunities improve facilitation patterns and edge-case resilience.

opportunities.title: No open-floor opening in Discovery
opportunities.description: Discovery jumps into structured categories without first inviting the user to dump everything they know. Forces Q&A interrogation instead of collaborative conversation.
opportunities.suggestion: Add one sentence before the category list: 'Begin Discovery with an open invitation for the user to share everything available — goals, documents, constraints, concerns, prior art — then identify which areas still need elicitation.'

opportunities.title: Headless input contract missing
opportunities.description: The contract defines the return schema but not the input schema. An automator cannot invoke headless mode without guessing what args to pass.
opportunities.suggestion: Add an 'Input Schema' section: required --sources (file paths), optional --mode (create/update/validate), optional --vague-terms. Include a minimal invocation example.

opportunities.title: Decision-log path absent from headless return
opportunities.description: Quality principles require headless returns to include the decision-log path for audit trail. Current schema omits it.
opportunities.suggestion: Add 'decision_log' field to the return schema.

opportunities.title: vague_terms merge chain broken for personal overrides
opportunities.description: The validation script reads _bmad/custom/hbc-create-requirements.toml directly, bypassing the BMad resolver. Personal .user.toml overrides are silently ignored.
opportunities.suggestion: Pass the resolved {workflow.vague_terms} as a --vague-terms CLI argument from SKILL.md instead of reading TOML directly in the script.

opportunities.title: vague_terms should be a TOML array, not comma-separated string
opportunities.description: TOML natively supports arrays. Comma-separated strings force manual parsing and are error-prone for override authors.
opportunities.suggestion: Change to vague_terms = ['fast', 'easy', ...]. Update CLI parsing to accept the resolved array. Keep --vague-terms CLI as comma-separated for ergonomics.

opportunities.title: No soft-gate transitions in Discovery
opportunities.description: Six discovery areas listed without guidance on transitioning between them. Users in flow get cut off; users needing more time get pushed forward.
opportunities.suggestion: Add: 'At each area boundary, use soft-gate elicitation — Anything else on [area], or shall we move on?'

opportunities.title: No compaction flush at Stages 3-4
opportunities.description: Stage 2 has explicit compaction flush but Stages 3 and 4 don't. If compaction fires after validation but before save, validation findings are lost.
opportunities.suggestion: Add compaction flush notes at end of Stages 3 and 4.

opportunities.title: Parallel-lens menu unexplained
opportunities.description: [A]/[P]/[C] appears without context. 'Party Mode' is an HBC convention new users won't know.
opportunities.suggestion: Add one-line parenthetical for each option.

opportunities.title: No import-and-convert path for experts
opportunities.description: Expert with pre-structured requirements has no way to skip Discovery. Neither create (from scratch) nor update (existing D-02) covers 'import and convert.'
opportunities.suggestion: Document in Stage 2 that when source documents contain structured requirements, Discovery should detect and skip to confirmation.

opportunities.title: Capture-don't-interrupt for cross-artifact insights
opportunities.description: Users frequently mention glossary terms or business-flow processes during requirements discovery. Nothing captures these for handoff.
opportunities.suggestion: Add: 'Silently capture glossary-worthy terms and business-flow processes — surface them in Stage 5 handoff.'

opportunities.title: Source inventory pre-pass script opportunity
opportunities.description: The LLM scans the project tree to discover existing documents — a deterministic operation costing 200-400 tokens per invocation.
opportunities.suggestion: A Python script scanning {project-root} for known HBC patterns, checking D-02 frontmatter, returning a compact JSON manifest.

opportunities.title: Validation script path not liftable to customize.toml
opportunities.description: The validation script path is hardcoded. Teams wanting a stricter validator cannot swap it without forking.
opportunities.suggestion: Add validation_script scalar to customize.toml with a comment about interface contract.

opportunities.title: Build-time decision log in references/
opportunities.description: Build-time decision log mixed with runtime-loaded content in references/. The dotfile prefix mitigates but doesn't eliminate ambiguity.
opportunities.suggestion: Acceptable as-is given dotfile convention. No action needed unless references/ gains more build artifacts.

opportunities.title: No graceful degradation when Python unavailable
opportunities.description: Skill requires Python 3.10+ for validation but doesn't specify fallback when unavailable.
opportunities.suggestion: Add: 'If validation script unavailable, fall back to LLM-only validation and note limitation in decision log.'

opportunities.title: Smart defaults from project-context.md
opportunities.description: project-context.md listed as source but skill doesn't say to pre-populate Discovery fields from it.
opportunities.suggestion: Add: 'Pre-populate Discovery fields from project-context.md where available; present as defaults for confirmation.'

opportunities.title: output_path naming ambiguity
opportunities.description: output_path points to a directory, not a file. Name is slightly ambiguous.
opportunities.suggestion: Rename to output_dir or document that this is a directory.

strengths: Right-sized SKILL.md at 96 lines / 1228 tokens — compact, no padding
Exemplary intelligence placement: deterministic validation in script, judgment in LLM
Validation script with 11 tests, auto_fixable flags, configurable vague terms
Minimal customization surface: 3 scalars covering real variance dimensions
Outcome-based prose throughout — stages describe goals, not step-by-step procedures
Compact resume/update handling in single natural block
Stage 2 compaction flush preserves discovery state across context compaction
Headless return contract is clean with clear status values and blocked reasons
Template structure aligns exactly with validation script checks
Script invocation syntax is exact in SKILL.md — fragile-operation done right
recommendations: Fix vague_terms merge chain — pass resolved value as CLI arg instead of reading TOML directly (O4)
Add headless input schema to the contract document (O2, O3)
Add open-floor opening to Discovery stage (O1)
Consider soft-gate transitions and capture-don't-interrupt patterns (O6, O10)
